title: [release-1.11-rhel] Fix ADD with URL
body:
Podman 1.6.4 and Buildah 1.11 in RHEL 7.9 had a fixed dragged
in accidentally to handle a Containerfile ADD command that had
a URL in it as part of a separate unrelated fix.  When we
cleaned up the repo recently, that fix was removed
as part of the cleanup effort as it had no corresponding
issue or BZ.

During testing, this issue popped up.  Now that we have a BZ to
attribute it to, putting the fix back in place "legally".

Fixes: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2112217

and backports:  https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2019 which
was spun up from this issue:  https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/4686

At the time of the fix, Buildah v1.7 was under development and the fix
was put into place there and for any newer versions.

Signed-off-by: tomsweeneyredhat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.11-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@cevich I think you fixed this in other branches, can it be fixed here too or did we run into obstacles?

```
[+0165s] E: The repository 'http://us-central1.gce.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu eoan Release' no longer has a Release file.
[+0165s] E: The repository 'http://us-central1.gce.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu eoan-updates Release' no longer has a Release file.
[+0165s] E: The repository 'http://us-central1.gce.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu eoan-backports Release' no longer has a Release file.
[+0165s] E: The repository 'http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu eoan Release' no longer has a Release file.
```

> I think you fixed this in other branches, can it be fixed here too or did we run into obstacles?

Oh man, the scripts here are sooooo ooooold, gosh.  Frankly I'm surprised CI was able to run at all.  This branch is not included in the set we run daily (to ensure CI keeps working).  Is there another release branch which is similar to this one?

If so, I'd guess that maybe we could pull both the images and the scripts from that one to here, but it's a crap-shoot if it will actually work or not.  Let me know if/how I can help.

@cevich, all the other release branches above this one have this fix in it without an issue.  Do you think there's any chance of getting the CI to be happy in this branch, or do we assume (oh oh) that it's fine, merge it here, and build a release for testing?
@cevich in theory, release-1.15 is the first branch with the fixes in it.  Do we steal from that?  FWIW, I"ll cc you onto an email stream, it's getting a fair amount of notice.

> in theory, release-1.15

The oldest branch I've been maintaining is release-1.16.  However, [it's trimmed down to basically just run the build and verify steps](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/5696376222), no actual testing :disappointed: I took at look at the CI scripts over there, and they too appear like they will suffer due to runtime package installs.

Since you're mostly concerned about RHEL-stuff anyway, maybe the solution is to just disable testing on Ubuntu?   The repos are gone so the release must be EOL.  It's not like there will ever be a release we care about happening there.  Would you like me to take care of that in another PR?  Also since (miraculously) CI is kind of working here, should I enable the cirrus-cron runs on this branch to keep it alive?

@cevich That's a good thought on just killing the Unbuntu bit as we are indeed focused on RHEL only for this fix and likely for this branch.  While you're at it, perhaps turning on the cirrus-cron to run on a monthly or weekly basis (your call) would not be a bad idea.

> While you're at it, perhaps turning on the cirrus-cron to run on a monthly or weekly basis (your call) would not be a bad idea.

Will do (this is easy, one-liner).
Just rebased to get the CI update from @cevich, fingers crossed for happy tests.
